title: Temperature
short: A measure of the average kinetic energy of a substance's particles, measured in kelvin (K).
full:
Temperature measures how hot or cold something is; microscopically it reflects the average kinetic energy of the random motion of the particles. Two bodies in thermal equilibrium have the same temperature.

Gas-law and efficiency formulas require the absolute (Kelvin) scale, where 0 K is absolute zero. Convert from Celsius with T[K] = T[°C] + 273.15. A difference of 1 K equals a difference of 1 °C.

The SI unit is the kelvin (K).
